title: acaena myriophylla
intro: Acaena myriophylla, also known as the woolly-headed caterpillar bur or woolly caterpillar bur, is a perennial plant species native to New Zealand, characterized by its woolly, greyish-green foliage and small yellow flowers. The medicinal properties of Acaena myriophylla are attributed to its anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. The plant's health benefits include its potential to treat digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory issues, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies. Acaena myriophylla is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
